export default "# Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Quick Reference\n\n## Part 2 - Definitions of Words and Terms\n\n**Acquisition**: The acquiring by contract with appropriated funds of supplies or services (including construction) by and for the use of the Federal Government through purchase or lease.\n\n**Contracting Officer (CO)**: A person with the authority to enter into, administer, and/or terminate contracts and make related determinations and findings.\n\n**Contracting Officer's Representative (COR)**: An individual designated and authorized in writing by the contracting officer to perform specific technical or administrative functions.\n\n## Part 15 - Contracting by Negotiation\n\n### 15.101 - Best Value Continuum\n- Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA)\n- Tradeoff Process (allows for higher prices for better technical solutions)\n\n### Key Principles:\n- Competition is encouraged\n- Proposals are evaluated against stated criteria\n- Awards are made to the offeror whose proposal provides the best value\n\n## Part 39 - Information Technology (IT) Acquisition\n\n### 39.103 - Modular Contracting\n- Break large IT acquisitions into smaller increments\n- Each increment should be useful and add value\n- Reduces risk and allows for course corrections\n\n## Agile Acquisition Best Practices\n\n1. **Incremental Funding**: Fund work in iterations or sprints\n2. **Firm-Fixed-Price per Iteration**: Reduces administrative burden\n3. **Continuous Stakeholder Engagement**: Regular demos and feedback\n4. **Quality Assurance Surveillance Plans (QASP)**: Focus on outcomes, not activities\n5. **Evaluation Criteria**: Consider technical challenges, oral presentations, confidence ratings\n\n## Resources\n- acquisition.gov - Official FAR website\n- TechFAR Hub - Guidance for agile IT acquisitions\n- Digital Services Playbook - Best practices from USDS\n"
